floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: `(0, 0x10)` is provably INERT - GCC 2.7.2 c-typeck.c internal_build_compound_expr returns the RHS at PARSE time for side-effect-free left operands (non-pedantic); `j -= 0x10` is tre

frontier.hypothesis: Replace `(0, 0x10)` with `0x10` at src/code6cac_c_mid.c:1235 and re-verify - expected byte-identical since the parser proof shows tree identity
frontier.mechanism: c-typeck.c internal_build_compound_expr parse-time discard (no GCC pass ever sees the comma)
frontier.next_probe: sandbox func_800392C8 --disable all with the plain `j -= 0x10`, then verify-oracle --rebuild; expect distance 0 and SHA1 == oracle

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Comma-expression cheat `j -= (0, 0x10)` in loop2: the left operand `0` is a constant with no side effects and is discarded by the C comma operator, making this semantically identical to `j -= 0x10`. Fails Test 1 (no semantic purpose — runtime behavior is byte-identical without the `0`), Test 2 (no human writes `(0, 0x10)` from a spec), and Test 3 (only justification is GCC-internal COMPOUND_EXPR tree coercion). Worker should: (1) confirm `j -= 0x10` produces identical asm — if yes, replace `(0, 0x10)` with `0x10` and re-verify oracle; (2) if `j -= 0x10` produces wrong asm, the comma expression is a genuine codegen coercion and a clean pure-C structural alternative must be found (e.g. restructuring the loop, adjusting variable types, or using a named intermediate) before this function can be COMPLETED-C.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
